rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in annex blocks within the appropriate AADL scope: component type for interface assumptions/guarantees, implementation for bindings.
Respect AADL scope boundaries: component type contracts reference only visible ports, implementation contracts reference subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers and scopes visible in the current AADL component or implementation.
Preserve AADL architecture structure during AGREE annex insertion.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the analyzed model.
Ensure AGREE clauses align with AADL component type and implementation boundaries.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Implementation-level assignments must reference valid subcomponent ports and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference subcomponent paths or ports not visible in the current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
